items.q: In Spain, how do you address a group of friends informally?
items.why: Students don't distinguish vosotros (informal plural, Spain) from ustedes (formal plural in Spain, but used for all plural in Latin America). In Spain: tú/vosotros = informal; usted/ustedes = formal. Latin America: only ustedes for plural.

items.q: Which preposition expresses purpose ('in order to')?
items.why: Students treat por and para as identical 'for'. PARA = destination, purpose, recipient, deadline. POR = reason/cause, exchange, duration, motion through. The distinction is regularly tested.

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to visit Spain' in Spanish?
items.why: Students drop the 'a' or use a conjugated verb. The near future is IR (conjugated) + A + INFINITIVE. The 'a' is obligatory: voy A comer, vamos A ver, vas A estudiar. Heavily tested for holiday/future plans.
The answer examiners want is: Argentina. “Mexico” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: 60. “20” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Year Ni una menos movement began?
items.why: Exact answer: 2015. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
